title: salix × forbyana
intro: Salix × forbyana, also known as Forby's willow or Forby's osier, is a hybrid willow plant that is a cross between Salix viminalis and Salix purpurea, resulting in a robust and adaptable shrub or small tree. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicylic acid and other anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Salix × forbyana include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe pain, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the bark of Salix × forbyana is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or consumed internally to harness its medicinal properties.
